This family consists of around 600 genera and 13,000 species, many of which are economically important for their medicinal, culinary, or ornamental values. As a member of this family, Secondatia arborea shares some common characteristics with other Rubiaceae species, such as its floral structure and leaf morphology. However, further research is required to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Secondatia arborea within the Rubiaceae family.
